summary: Fabric v2.0 rc.2 preparation work locked through grill-me session: 8 tasks across 5 phases (A→B→C→D→E) covering rc.1 dead-code archaeology, dropped-client removal, schema forward-compat, self-repo dogfood + 8-decision capture, and release artifacts (tag + GitHub prerelease, NOT npm). Total budget ~10-15h (~2-3 work days). Output is a tagged v2.0.0-rc.1 GitHub prerelease with v2 baseline knowledge entries committed to repo.
approach: Phase A (commits A/B/C/D — code archaeology in 4 atomic layer-isolated commits: CLI commands, server services, templates, tests) → Phase B (drop Windsurf/RooCode/GeminiCLI clients with strict-union enforcement) → Phase C (last-cheap-window schema additions: tags field + 4 event-type renames + 3 deletions + docs/schema.md) → Phase D (self-repo dogfood — fabric runs init on itself, plus author 8 KT-D decision baseline entries) → Phase E (version bump 2.0.0-rc.1, CHANGELOG, README 5-line banner, annotated tag, GitHub prerelease — no npm publish). Phase ordering is forced by dependencies: A blocks B (B's strict-reject test relies on A's removal of legacy paths); C blocks D (schema must be locked before dogfood produces baseline); D blocks E (release tag should include dogfood baseline as part of rc.1). Within Phase A, TASK-001/002/003 are parallel-safe (no file overlap: cli-commands / server-services / templates) and TASK-004 (tests) merges all three. TASK-005..008 are strictly sequential.

design_decisions.decision: Q1 — Version strategy: bump package.json to 2.0.0-rc.1 (single commit, retroactive on rc.1 commit aaf9c2c); annotated git tag `v2.0.0-rc.1`; old `1.8.0-rc.3` stays in npm history (deprecate as needed); NOT publish to npm during entire rc.x cycle
design_decisions.rationale: Keep npm `latest` dist-tag clean for stable users (no `next` pollution); GitHub-only RC release provides public download for testers without disrupting npm consumers; `2.0.0` stable will publish to npm `latest` after rc cycle ends
design_decisions.tradeoff: RC adopters must install via git ref instead of `pnpm add fabric@next` — slight friction acceptable for prerelease cohort

design_decisions.decision: Q2 — Dropped client cleanup: independent commit removing Windsurf / Roo Code / Gemini CLI from supported clients; drop `.passthrough()` from fabric-config Zod schema; tighten ClientKind to strict union `claudeCode | cursor | codex`; unknown keys reject and exit (no warn-and-continue)
design_decisions.rationale: Memory: Fabric scope locked to Claude Code / Cursor / Codex CLI only; loud failure prevents silent mis-config in customer projects; clean-slate preference (zero users → no migration tax)
design_decisions.tradeoff: Any customer with windsurf|rooCode|geminiCLI entry in fabric-config gets hard error on next `fab` run instead of warning — accepted per zero-user clean-slate decision

design_decisions.decision: Q3 — Self-repo dogfood: two-step (init pipeline + 8 KT-D decision authoring) combined into single commit `chore(dogfood): v2.0 baseline + key decisions`; bug-handling protocol: hotfixes go on `2.0.0-rc.1` tag as `2.0.0-rc.1.fix.N`, NOT rolled into rc.2 scope
design_decisions.rationale: Atomic commit shows 'fabric works on itself' as one shippable unit; pre-release dogfood validates init pipeline on a real-world non-trivial repo (not just test fixtures); bug-isolation protocol keeps rc.2 scope clean
design_decisions.tradeoff: Combined commit is larger (~10 new files) but reverting is also atomic

design_decisions.decision: Q4 — rc.1 真正完工 in 4 atomic commits (A/B/C/D): A=CLI command layer (delete bootstrap.ts, kill buildInitialTaxonomyMarkdown + .fabric/rules paths in init/scan/forensic); B=server services layer (rename rule-meta-builder, kill v1 doctor checks, retarget watcher to .fabric/knowledge/**, delete bootstrap-guide.ts); C=templates (rewrite AGENTS.md.template, delete variants/+husky/+fabric-init three-piece); D=tests (delete 4 v1-concept test files, adapt fixtures)
design_decisions.rationale: Code review missed v1.x dead code paths — concrete grep findings showed buildInitialTaxonomyMarkdown still in init.ts, INITIAL_TAXONOMY in 5 files, .fabric/rules/ in 26 files including user-facing templates; layer-isolated commits enable parallel work (A1/A2/A3 parallel-safe) and clean revertability
design_decisions.tradeoff: 4 commits instead of 1 — slight ceremony cost for much cleaner history and parallel execution

design_decisions.decision: Q5 — Release hygiene: CHANGELOG entry with 4 subsections (BREAKING + Removed + Added + Coming next); annotated git tag `v2.0.0-rc.1`; GitHub release `prerelease: true`; NOT publish to npm during rc.x phase; README.md gets 5-line v2 banner injected at top (NOT wait for rc.4 full rewrite)
design_decisions.rationale: 5-line banner is cheap and prevents misorientation for anyone landing on README during RC cycle; full README rewrite stays rc.4 scope but landing page deserves immediate truth-in-advertising
design_decisions.tradeoff: README will be re-rewritten in rc.4 — banner gets replaced/integrated but cost is negligible

design_decisions.decision: Q6 — Schema forward-compat (last cheap window, single commit): add 1 frontmatter field `tags: [tag1, tag2]` (flat flow-style array); rename 4 event types (rule_*→knowledge_*); delete 3 event types (rule_baseline_accepted, baseline_synced, legacy_client_path_present); keep 12 unchanged; net 19→13 event types; write `docs/schema.md` 1-page contract doc. NOT add: last_review_at, source_session, confidence_score, review_count, parent_id (let events.jsonl be single source of truth)
design_decisions.rationale: Window cost: rc.1 baseline doesn't exist yet → no fixture migration cost; after rc.2 dogfood emits real entries, rename gets expensive; events.jsonl as single source of truth avoids double-write consistency bugs; rc.3 review skill needs tags field for filter search — must land before dogfood populates baseline
design_decisions.tradeoff: NOT adding parent_id/source_session means rc.3 review skill must derive lineage from events.jsonl (more complex query, but consistent)

design_decisions.decision: Q7 — Execution sequence: A→B→C→D→E forced by dependencies; Phase D decision capture uses option B (8 entries, full grill-me decision tree coverage) instead of option A (5 entries); Day-1 gate before opening rc.2 includes 5 categories of bash checks (code archaeology / build+test+lint / schema additions / dogfood / release artifacts) — ALL must pass
design_decisions.rationale: A blocks B (B's strict-reject test relies on A's removal of legacy paths); C blocks D (schema must be locked before dogfood produces baseline); D blocks E (release tag should include dogfood baseline as part of rc.1); option B captures complete grill-me decision tree (no orphan rationale); Day-1 gate prevents premature rc.2 opening on unstable foundation
design_decisions.tradeoff: Option B (8 entries) takes slightly more authoring effort than option A (5) — accepted for completeness

_metadata.scope_boundary: rc.2 PREPARATION ONLY — does NOT include rc.2 implementation (fab_extract_knowledge MCP / fabric-archive skill / Stop hooks)
out_of_scope: Full README rewrite + docs/knowledge-types.md + docs/initialization.md + docs/roadmap.md (rc.4)
New event types knowledge.{proposed,promoted,layer_changed,demoted,archived,archive_attempted} — rc.2/3/4 will add as z.literal() discriminated union variants
npm publish for any rc.x version (deferred to 2.0.0 stable)

A.label: rc.1 真正完工 (code archaeology, 4 atomic commits)

E.label: Release artifacts (version + CHANGELOG + tag + GitHub prerelease + README banner)

_metadata.day_1_gate_source: planning-context.md §Q7 — code archaeology grep + build/test/lint + schema additions + dogfood + release artifacts (all must pass before opening rc.2)

quality_check.method: self-review against planning-context.md and required structure

validated_dimensions.completeness: 8 tasks map exactly to 9 commits (TASK-007 combines 2 commits per Q3); all grill-me Q1-Q7 decisions captured
validated_dimensions.granularity: Each task is an atomic commit boundary; estimated 1-3h each (within 15-60 min × parallel-thread or per-developer day budget)
validated_dimensions.dependencies: DAG validated: 3-way fan-out (001/002/003) → join (004) → linear (005→006→007→008); no circular deps
validated_dimensions.convergence_criteria: All 8 tasks have quantified, grep/test-verifiable convergence criteria sourced from Day-1 gate
validated_dimensions.implementation_steps: All tasks have ≥4 implementation steps with specific commands or actions
validated_dimensions.constraint_compliance: Aligns with memory constraints (Fabric supported clients = Claude Code/Cursor/Codex CLI; clean-slate preference); no specs/*.md found in `ccw spec load --category planning` so no further spec gate
